agenda: mechanism seams in the existing worktree pipeline (which script each R touches)
component-design.decision: Extend the existing worktree-reviewer pipeline at named seams (no new architecture). Component-to-responsibility: worktree-reviewer.ps1 owns the hard timeout (R5); worktree-review-orchestrator.ps1 owns partial harvest + evidence labelling (R1/R4); the reviewer instruction contract owns incremental emission (R1); reviewer-selection-policy + reviewer-authorization-gate own pre-flight independence + labelled same-host fallback (R3); the navigator/provider own the remediation-menu surface (R2/R3/R6/accept/override); co-review-round-state.json carries the human choice to the next run; review-signoff-evidence-gate owns tiered degraded-evidence + recorded ack + degraded-block override (R4/D5). R6 remediation menu (more time / different host / narrow scope code-process-file-function / accept partial / override block) is the unifying surface; scope-narrowing extends the existing user-code/subtree scoping.

agenda: degraded-evidence gate policy (D4, tiered by actual assurance)
blocking-finding handling from a degraded review (D5)
requirements-nfr.decision: Guiding NFR: any review > nothing; the gate never hard-deadlocks. D1 hybrid interaction (R3 pre-flight with labelled same-host fallback; R2 post-hoc with a pre-flight generous-budget heuristic for large diffs). D4 tiered evidence: full+independent auto-allows at any budget; partial OR same-host needs a recorded first-class ack verdict; time-extended is not reduced assurance. D5 blocking findings always surface and stop; a degraded-review block is overridable with a recorded reason. D3 hard timeout = independent watchdog + process-group/job-object tree-kill + stdio-redirect + 5s graceful flush; WSL-validation is a hard acceptance gate.
